role_summary: Defines the foundational functional requirements, user journeys, and deterministic pipeline architecture for an automated code analysis and documentation generation system, serving as the primary specification baseline for development, validation, and traceability.
extracted_finding: The system implements a deterministic, four-stage automated pipeline for repository analysis and documentation generation. Core functional responsibilities include: (1) Intelligent traversal with configurable path/size filters to exclude non-essential assets and generate structural introspection assessments; (2) Granular extraction that translates technical code into immutable, timestamped domain-concept notes while preserving raw evidence for ambiguous data; (3) Section synthesis that aggregates notes into consistent documentation using both schema-validated structured outputs and free-form narrative generation; (4) Cross-cutting derivation that maps multi-component relationships into behavioral narratives and interaction diagrams; and (5) Execution reporting that consolidates pipeline health metrics, inclusion/exclusion statistics, and chronological provenance tracking. Key business rules mandate strict adherence to traversal boundaries, immutable note creation post-extraction, deterministic stage-gated progression, and full traceability from final documentation back to source artifacts. Documented gaps highlight undefined heuristics for artifact classification, contradictory insight reconciliation, explicit note-to-section mapping, workflow serialization schemas, and access control/rate-limiting policies.
